# youtrack_role_assignment (Resource)

YouTrack role assignment resource. This resource manages role assignments with ONLY global scope in YouTrack, which assigns a role to a user or group.

## Example Usage

```terraform
terraform {
  required_providers {
    youtrack = {
      source = "registry.opentofu.org/elcait/youtrack"
    }
  }
}

provider "youtrack" {
  base_url = "https://youtrack.example.com"
  token    = "your-api-token"
}

# Create a role
resource "youtrack_role" "developer" {
  name        = "Developer"
  description = "Developer role"
  permissions = ["Read Issue", "Create Issue"]
}

# Assign the role to a user with global scope
resource "youtrack_role_assignment" "example" {
  role_id      = youtrack_role.developer.id
  holder_login = "john.doe" # Username or group name
  holder_type  = "user"     # Optional: 'user' or 'group'
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `holder_login` (String) The login (username) of the user or name of the group to assign the role to
- `role_id` (String) The ID of the role to assign (e.g. youtrack_role.my_role.id)

### Optional

- `holder_type` (String) The type of the holder: 'user' or 'group'. If not specified, will try user first, then group.

### Read-Only

- `holder_id` (String) The ID of the holder (computed)
- `holder_name` (String) The name of the holder (computed)
- `id` (String) YouTrack ID of the role assignment (computed)
- `role_name` (String) The name of the assigned role (computed)

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
#!/bin/sh
# Import a role assignment by its ID
terraform import youtrack_role_assignment.example "role-assignment-id"
```
